an1.219:1.1: "This, *bhikkhus*, is the foremost among my *bhikkhu* disciples who are learned — namely, Ānanda.
an1.221:1.1: … Among those of great attainment — namely, Ānanda.
an1.222:1.1: … Among those of fortitude — namely, Ānanda.
an1.224:1.1: … Among those with a great assembly — namely, Uruvelakassapa.
an1.225:1.1: … Among those who inspire confidence in families — namely, Kāḷudāyī.
an1.226:1.1: … Among those of few ailments — namely, Bākula.
an1.227:1.1: … Among those who recollect past lives — namely, Sobhita.
an1.228:1.1: … Among those who hold the *vinaya* — namely, Upāli.
an1.229:1.1: … Among those who instruct *bhikkhunīs* — namely, Nandaka.
an1.230:1.1: … Among those who guard the doors of the *indriyas* — namely, Nanda.
an1.231:1.1: … Among those who instruct *bhikkhus* — namely, Mahākappina.
an1.232:1.1: … Among those skilled in the fire element — namely, Sāgata.
an1.233:1.1: … Among those of ready wit — namely, Rādha.
an1.234:1.1: … Among those who wear coarse robes — namely, Mogharājā."
